claim: The manuscript uses a simple substitution cipher encoding abbreviated medieval Latin
evidence_against: Decipherments produced largely unintelligible text. Other researchers could not reproduce meaningful readings using his key. Statistical properties of Voynich text do not match abbreviated Latin.

tests_run.details: Decipherments produced largely unintelligible text. Other researchers could not reproduce meaningful readings using his key. Statistical properties of Voynich text do not match abbreviated Latin.

reasoning: Historical attempt by Joseph Martin Feely (1943). Cipher type: simple substitution. Proposed language: abbreviated Latin.
source: Feely, J.M. 'Roger Bacon's Cipher: The Right Key Found' (1943); D'Imperio (1978)
